name: Aranjuez – circular until Cortijo de San Isidro
name_original: Aranjuez – circular hasta el Cortijo de San Isidro
region_intro: Touring cycling around Aranjuez features a unique cultural landscape shaped by the Tagus and Jarama rivers, offering a blend of natural beauty and historical grandeur. The region is characterized by a fertile valley, extensive royal gardens, and a surrounding countryside with agricultural lands. Routes often include gentle slopes and diverse scenery, ranging from paved paths within the town to unpaved gravel tracks in the wider area. Best touring cycling routes around Aranjuez The most popular touring cycling route is Royal Palace of Aranjuez – Royal Palace of Aranjuez loop from Aranjuez, a 7.0 miles (11.3 km) trail that takes 44 minutes to complete. This route offers views of the Royal Palace and its surrounding gardens. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Aranjuez – circular until Cortijo de San Isidro, an easy 15.7 miles (25.3 km) path. This circular route explores the area around Aranjuez, passing through varied landscapes. Local touring cyclists also love the Gravel Track to Titulcia – Plaza Mayor de Titulcia loop from Aranjuez, a 23.5 miles (37.8 km) trail leading through gravel tracks and riverside areas, often completed in about 3 hours 7 minutes. Touring cycling around Aranjuez is defined by royal gardens, the confluence of the Tagus and Jarama rivers, and a rich agricultural landscape. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy rides through the gardens to more challenging routes in the countryside. The routes in Aranjuez are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 24 reviews. More than 400 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Aranjuez's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Aranjuez?
A: There are over 50 touring cycling routes documented around Aranjuez, offering a wide range of options for different skill levels and preferences. You'll find everything from easy rides through the royal gardens to more challenging routes in the surrounding countryside.

Q: Are there easy or family-friendly touring cycling routes in Aranjuez?
A: Yes, Aranjuez offers several easy touring cycling routes perfect for families and beginners. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/21957356" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Royal Palace of Aranjuez – Royal Palace of Aranjuez loop from Aranjuez&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 11.3 km ride that takes you through the beautiful palace grounds. Another great option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e962937798/aranjuez-circular-until-cortijo-de-san-isidro" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aranjuez – circular until Cortijo de San Isidro&lt;/a&gt;, an easy 25.3 km path exploring varied landscapes.

Q: What kind of scenery can I expect on touring cycling routes in Aranjuez?
A: Touring cycling routes in Aranjuez offer diverse scenery, blending historical grandeur with natural beauty. You'll cycle through the extensive royal gardens, along the Tagus and Jarama rivers, and past agricultural lands like vineyards, olive groves, and melon farms in the surrounding countryside. The region's unique cultural landscape, recognized by UNESCO, provides a harmonious mix of water, vegetation, and architecture.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in Aranjuez?
A: Yes, many touring cycling routes in Aranjuez are designed as loops, allowing you to start and end in the same location. An example is the easy &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e962937798/aranjuez-circular-until-cortijo-de-san-isidro" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aranjuez – circular until Cortijo de San Isidro&lt;/a&gt;, which is 25.3 km long. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/21957356" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Royal Palace of Aranjuez – Royal Palace of Aranjuez loop from Aranjuez&lt;/a&gt;, a shorter 11.3 km ride.

Q: What are some notable attractions or landmarks to see along the cycling routes?
A: Aranjuez is rich in historical and natural attractions. Many routes pass by the majestic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4576777" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Royal Palace of Aranjuez&lt;/a&gt; and its stunning gardens, including the Jardín del Príncipe and Jardín de la Isla. You might also encounter charming towns like Chinchón with its medieval square, or explore areas near the Tagus and Jarama rivers. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3022680" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Titulcia Main Square&lt;/a&gt; is another point of interest on some routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/3962131" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gravel Track to Titulcia – Plaza Mayor de Titulcia loop from Aranjuez&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Aranjuez?
A: The mild climate of Aranjuez makes it suitable for touring cycling for much of the year. Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for exploring the gardens and countryside. Even in winter, options are available, though it's always advisable to check local weather conditions before heading out.

Q: Are there any challenging touring cycling routes for experienced riders?
A: Yes, for more experienced touring cyclists seeking a challenge, there are routes with greater distances and elevation changes. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/1285746" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Apollo's Fountain – Casa del Labrador loop from Aranjuez&lt;/a&gt; covers 60.2 km with moderate difficulty, featuring significant elevation gain. Another moderate option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38187359" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Plaza Mayor de Chinchón – Villaconejos loop from Tarraco&lt;/a&gt;, which is 44.7 km long.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about the routes in Aranjuez?
A: The touring cycling routes in Aranjuez are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 24 reviews. Cyclists often praise the unique blend of historical sites, the beauty of the royal gardens, and the peaceful riverside paths along the Tagus and Jarama rivers.

Q: Can I access touring cycling routes in Aranjuez using public transport?
A: Aranjuez is accessible by public transport, particularly by train from Madrid, making it convenient to reach the starting points of many cycling routes. Once in Aranjuez, many routes begin directly from the town center or are easily reachable, allowing you to explore without needing a car.

Q: Are there places to park if I bring my own bike to Aranjuez?
A: Yes, Aranjuez has various parking options available within the town, especially near the Royal Palace and other main attractions. These parking areas can serve as convenient starting points for your touring cycling adventures, allowing you to easily unload your bike and begin your ride.

Q: What types of surfaces can I expect on the touring cycling routes?
A: The touring cycling routes around Aranjuez feature a mix of surfaces. You'll find paved paths within the town and royal gardens, as well as unpaved gravel tracks in the wider countryside. This variety allows for different cycling experiences, from smooth rides to more adventurous off-road sections.
tips: The Royal Chapel of San Isidro was built by order of King Carlos III because the oratory of the Royal House could not accommodate all of its employees and laborers who lived in the Cortijo. In this way, the ministers Floridablanca and Grimaldi commissioned the project to Jaime Marquet, but when he died in 1782, Manuel Serrano took over building the current church, which was inaugurated on May 15, 1788. The chapel has a neoclassic. The portico is adorned with four Doric style columns.
As it passes through Aranjuez, the Tagus River has a recreational use for the practice of multiple rowing sports.

One of the Royal Sites of the Spanish monarchy since Philip II named it in 1560, it has also had the title of Villa since 1899. Therefore, it is known as Royal Site and Villa of Aranjuez. Declared a Cultural Landscape Heritage of Humanity by UNESCO in 2001.
